heading: Demonstration health projects

children.heading: .—Purpose
children.label: a .—Purpose
children.text: To demonstrate the value of adequate health facilities and services to the economic development of the region, a Commission may make grants for the planning, construction, equipment, and operation of demonstration health, nutrition, and child care projects to serve distressed areas (referred to in this section as a “demonstration health project”), including hospitals, regional health diagnostic and treatment centers, and other facilities and services necessary for the purposes of this section.
an entity described in section 15501(a);
an institution of higher education (as defined in section 101(a) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 ());20 U.S.C. 1001(a)
a hospital (as defined in section 1861 of the Social Security Act ()); or42 U.S.C. 1395x
a critical access hospital (as defined in that section).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: A Commission may make grants for planning expenses necessary for the development and operation of demonstration health projects for the region served by the Commission.

children.heading: .—Maximum commission contribution
children.label: 2 .—Maximum commission contribution
children.text: The maximum Commission contribution for a demonstration health project that receives a grant under paragraph (1) shall be made in accordance with section 15501(d).

children.heading: .—Sources of assistance
children.label: 3 .—Sources of assistance
children.text: A grant under paragraph (1) may be provided entirely from amounts made available to carry out this section or in combination with amounts provided under other Federal grant programs.

children.heading: .—Federal share for grants under other federal grant programs
children.label: 4 .—Federal share for grants under other federal grant programs
children.text: Notwithstanding any provision of law limiting the Federal share in other Federal grant programs, amounts made available to carry out this subsection may be used to increase the Federal share of another Federal grant up to the maximum contribution described in paragraph (2).

children.label: A
children.text: costs of construction;

children.label: i
children.text: not operated for profit; or

children.label: ii
children.text: previously operated for profit if the Commission finds that health services would not otherwise be provided in the area served by the facility if the acquisition is not made; and

children.label: C
children.text: the acquisition of initial equipment.

children.label: A
children.text: shall be approved in accordance with section 15503; and

children.label: B
children.text: shall not be incompatible with the applicable provisions of title VI of the Public Health Service Act ( et seq.), the Developmental Disabilities Assistance and Bill of Rights Act of 2000 ( et seq.), and other laws authorizing grants for the construction of health-related facilities, without regard to any provisions in those laws relating to appropriation authorization ceilings or to allotments among the States.42 U.S.C. 29142 U.S.C. 15001

children.heading: .—Maximum commission contribution
children.label: 3 .—Maximum commission contribution
children.text: The maximum Commission contribution for a demonstration health project that receives a grant under paragraph (1) shall be made in accordance with section 15501(d).

children.heading: .—Sources of assistance
children.label: 4 .—Sources of assistance
children.text: A grant under paragraph (1) may be provided entirely from amounts made available to carry out this section or in combination with amounts provided under other Federal grant programs.

children.heading: .—Contribution to increased federal share for other federal grants
children.label: 5 .—Contribution to increased federal share for other federal grants
children.text: Notwithstanding any provision of law limiting the Federal share in another Federal grant program for the construction or equipment of a demonstration health project, amounts made available to carry out this subsection may be used to increase Federal grants for component facilities of a demonstration health project to a maximum of 90 percent of the cost of the facilities.

children.label: A
children.text: the costs of operation of the facility; and

children.label: B
children.text: initial operating costs, including the costs of attracting, training, and retaining qualified personnel.

children.label: A
children.text: publicly owned;

children.label: B
children.text: owned by a public or private nonprofit organization;

children.label: C
children.text: a private hospital described in section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 and exempt from taxation under section 501(a) of that Code; or

children.label: D
children.text: a private hospital that provides a certain amount of uncompensated care, as determined by the Commission, and applies for the grant in partnership with a State, local government, or Indian Tribe.

children.heading: .—Maximum commission contribution
children.label: 3 .—Maximum commission contribution
children.text: The maximum Commission contribution for a demonstration health project that receives a grant under paragraph (1) shall be made in accordance with section 15501(d).

children.heading: .—Sources of assistance
children.label: 4 .—Sources of assistance
children.text: A grant under paragraph (1) may be provided entirely from amounts made available to carry out this section or in combination with amounts provided under other Federal grant programs for the operation of health-related facilities or the provision of health and child development services, including parts A and B of title IV and title XX of the Social Security Act ( et seq., 621 et seq., 1397 et seq.).42 U.S.C. 601

children.heading: .—Federal share
children.label: 5 .—Federal share
children.text: Notwithstanding any provision of law limiting the Federal share in the other Federal programs described in paragraph (4), amounts made available to carry out this subsection may be used to increase the Federal share of a grant under those programs up to the maximum contribution described in paragraph (3).
addiction treatment and access to resources helping individuals in recovery;
workforce shortages in the healthcare industry; or
access to services for screening and diagnosing chronic health issues.
